briefing.headline: Rig up a Ned Rig — steady summer approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Ned Rig is a strong choice for summer with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: In stained water, use a chartreuse jig head on the traditional Ned rig for added visual edge with a 3-inch stick bait.

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In stained water, use a chartreuse jig head on the traditional Ned rig for added visual edge with a 3-inch stick bait.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Texas rig a Ned bait on a swing head jig to fish snaggy areas where an exposed hook empties your terminal box.
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: When fishing ledges, keep baits on bottom but often burn them fast then kill the retrieve to trigger reaction strikes — this produced key fish at Chickamauga

Hackney: 'Quite a few fish live shallow even during the hottest time of year. They hold in shade of boat docks, overhanging trees, lily pads, and grass. Hot weather triggers grass growth that creates shade, oxygen, and ambush points.'

KVD: In summer on grass lakes, big fish move offshore to the middle of the lake where the bait is. They don't stay near shore when it's hot. Look for scattered hydrilla clumps in the middle of the lake.

Palaniuk: 'Big fish that haven't made the full transition to main summertime structure are singles or doubles on brushpiles or standing timber with a bottom composition change (mud to hard), on straight banks leading from spawning areas to points ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable summer patterns are predictable. Dawn/dusk fish are shallow (3-8ft) near cover. Midday fish pull to offshore structure at 12-25ft — ledges, humps, brush piles, and creek channel bends.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
